methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Argotec is a Growth-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by David Avino, currently targeting €75M in capital raise. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space.

Standard triangulation methods prove inapplicable here. Peer-Median benchmarking cannot proceed: while 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, zero satisfy narrow-stage and similar-valuation-tier filters simultaneously; the analysis requires a minimum of 3 datapoints to avoid misleading results. Public-Comp Anchoring is similarly constrained: the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 25.8x P/S across the 6-ticker set, but Argotec's revenue remains undisclosed, precluding multiple application.

Without disclosed financials or sufficiently clustered peer transactions at comparable stage and valuation tier, traditional quantitative anchoring cannot be supported. Any valuation conclusion at this stage would depend critically on: (1) Argotec's current-year revenue and growth trajectory; (2) comparable transaction multiples from the private peer set (SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs cohort), which are not provided here; (3) cash-burn profile and runway relative to the €75M target. The analyst should request these inputs before proceeding to a defensible range.
